raw: {"trigger_ref": "019db473-61e1-7001-98a8-bc0afd8108f0", "tenant_id": "019db473-61e1-7001-98a8-bc0afd8108f0", "claim_ops": [{"op": "insert", "entry": {"born_from_event_id": "019db473-61e1-7001-98a8-bc0afd8108f0", "proposition": {"kind": "state", "subject": "Globex Inc", "assertion": "Globex is actively evaluating two alternatives this quarter and renewal in November is not a given unless pricing and feature gaps are resolved."}, "natural": "Marcus from Globex Inc has stated they are actively evaluating two competing vendors this quarter and that renewal in November is contingent on resolving pricing and the two feature gaps (reporting export performance and SAML SSO).", "confidence": 0.85, "scope_actors": [], "scope_entities": [], "scope_temporal": {"valid_from": "2026-04-22T09:08:51.663467+00:00", "valid_until": null}, "falsifier": {"kind": "observation_pattern", "pattern": "Globex confirms renewal or stops evaluating alternatives without pricing/feature resolution", "within_window": "P90D"}}}], "reasoning_trace": "The triggering event is an email from Marcus (VP Ops, Globex Inc) stating: (1) team frustration with slow reporting export and unaddressed SAML setup, (2) pressure from finance to consolidate vendors before November renewal, (3) actively evaluating two alternatives this quarter, (4) renewal not a given unless pricing AND feature gaps are addressed. The existing models already capture: the feature requests (019db473-6005-7000-8b3a), the precarious renewal state (019db473-6000-7000-be1b), and the churn-risk concern (019db473-359c-7000-9a53). However, none of the existing models capture the specific new fact that Marcus explicitly stated they are actively evaluating two alternatives this quarter AND that renewal is contingent on pricing + feature gaps — this is a stronger, more specific assertion than the existing concern models.
